title: silene longicilia subsp. cintrana
intro: Silene longicilia subsp. cintrana, also known as the Cintra silene or cintrana silene, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a rare and endemic plant found in the Azores islands, Portugal, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Silene longicilia subsp. cintrana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and other infections.
